evidence: Lightbox flow is split across multi-layer adapters with very wide contracts: src/shared/components/MediaLightbox/ImageLightbox.tsx builds a large shared input object and then re-expands state into multiple render models; src/shared/components/MediaLightbox/hooks/useSharedLightboxState.ts defines broad nested input/output surfaces that bundle many unrelated concerns.

Boundary translation is repeated in multiple call sites instead of stabilized once: src/shared/components/MediaGallery/components/MediaGalleryLightbox.tsx decomposes and reassembles grouped contracts before passing many props into MediaLightbox, and src/tools/travel-between-images/components/Timeline.tsx passes another large prop matrix into the same lightbox surface.

Supabase edge handlers repeat similar request choreography (bootstrap, auth branching, ownership checks, logging/flush, response shaping) across multiple files rather than sharing an executable request policy layer: supabase/functions/generate-upload-url/index.ts, supabase/functions/get-task-output/index.ts, supabase/functions/update-shot-pair-prompts/index.ts.

Cross-layer business filtering is copy-carried from timeline behavior into edge code: supabase/functions/update-shot-pair-prompts/index.ts comments that filtering must match Timeline logic, but keeps its own implementation; drift is visible in the mismatched identifier use (`enhanced_prompts` vs `enhancedPrompts`).

findings.summary: Shared lightbox abstraction has become a god-facade with mixed responsibilities and high coupling.
evidence: useSharedLightboxState exposes nested sections for variants/navigation/star/references/lineage/shots/source generation/layout/button groups in one hook contract, while ImageLightbox still performs major orchestration and transformation before and after calling it.

ImageLightbox composes multiple model builders (shared state, edit orchestrator, workflow bar/controls, layout model) and passes large callback/state bundles downstream, indicating no single stable ownership boundary for lightbox behavior.
findings.suggestion: Split lightbox composition into explicit domain slices with narrow contracts (for example: `useLightboxNavigationModel`, `useLightboxShotActions`, `useLightboxPanelModel`) and keep one integration layer that only composes slice outputs. Reduce cross-slice callback passing by introducing a typed action interface shared by panel/workflow presenters.

findings.summary: MediaLightbox integration is duplicated via large prop-tunneling adapters across features.
evidence: MediaGalleryLightbox defines many grouped contracts (core/navigation/actions/shotWorkflow/ui/taskModal/taskData/integration), then destructures and remaps them into a long MediaLightbox prop call.

Timeline independently assembles another large MediaLightbox prop matrix (navigation, shot workflow, task details, optimistic state, transition hooks), duplicating boundary translation logic with slightly different conventions.
findings.suggestion: Create a single feature-agnostic `MediaLightboxInputAdapter` shape and factory helpers per source context (gallery/timeline/tool pages). Keep MediaLightbox invocation centralized so call sites provide one typed adapter object instead of dozens of direct props.

findings.summary: Edge handlers duplicate auth/ownership/request orchestration despite shared runtime utilities.
evidence: Each handler repeats similar flow: bootstrap, parse/validate body fields, branch on service-role vs user token, perform ownership checks, emit structured error responses, and manually flush logger on many exits.
A parallel shared request utility exists (`edgeRequest.ts`) but handlers primarily use a separate bootstrap path, leaving duplicated behavior spread across endpoints and increasing drift risk.
findings.suggestion: Consolidate endpoint choreography into one shared request pipeline (`withEdgeRequest`) that standardizes method/body/auth/ownership/error response steps and exposes only endpoint-specific business logic hooks.

findings.summary: Timeline filtering rules are copy-forked into edge logic and already drifting.
evidence: update-shot-pair-prompts implements its own "must match Timeline" image filtering (video-type and timeline_frame rules) instead of reusing a shared predicate/contract, then adds custom extension checks and sentinel handling locally.
The same block contains a mismatched identifier in the skip reason branch (`enhanced_prompts` vs `enhancedPrompts`), showing exactly the kind of drift introduced by copy-carried logic.
findings.suggestion: Extract a shared, tested shot-generation eligibility rule module used by both timeline-facing UI and edge handlers (or define a canonical DB-side view/RPC). Replace local ad-hoc checks and fix the identifier mismatch to remove runtime/reference risk.
